codeReview.summary: The feature stays in scope and adds the requested compat suite, but its continuation assertions are wired to the internal follow-up queue instead of the user-visible injected-message surface required by the mission contract. That means the tests can pass even when continuation never lands in session history or drives the next assistant turn, so the end-to-end compatibility coverage is incomplete.

issues.description: The suite introduces `getQueuedContinuationMessages()` on top of `harness.session.getFollowUpMessages()` and uses it for the positive-path compatibility assertions (lines 277, 309, 324-325, 349, and 376 in commit `66aa4ee2`). Mission AGENTS requires `harness.getInjectedUserMessages()` for continuation integration tests, and the validation contract requires observing a follow-up user message plus a subsequent assistant turn (`VAL-CROSS-004`) rather than only an internal queued follow-up. As written, these tests still pass when continuation is queued but never becomes a visible injected user message or never runs the next turn, so the feature does not fully cover the specified behavior.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker marked `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` as true, but the reviewed commit bypasses the mission's canonical continuation observability path by asserting on `session.getFollowUpMessages()` instead of `harness.getInjectedUserMessages()`. The extension-worker skill would benefit from explicitly restating that continuation integration tests must use the harness helper, not queue internals, when a feature depends on runtime follow-up behavior.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Mission guidance: `/Users/yeongyu/.factory/missions/604eaa29-2cf3-4c01-af2e-35ceedb95304/AGENTS.md:92`. Reviewed code: `packages/coding-agent/test/suite/todotools-continuation-compat.test.ts` lines 135-137 and 277/309/324-325/349/376 in commit `66aa4ee2`. Transcript skeleton still reports `handoff.skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true` for worker session `49e7c7b0-a3c0-4f1c-b604-1cf360da6485`.

summary: Fail: the compat suite validates queued follow-up state rather than the required injected user-message/end-to-end continuation surface, so it does not fully satisfy the continuation compatibility contract.
